Piper nigrum, commonly known as black pepper, is a flowering vine in the family Piperaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurveda, due to its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Piper nigrum include piperine, a compound responsible for its pungency and medicinal properties, as well as other alkaloids such as pipernonaline and piperonaline. The most common herbal preparations made with Piper nigrum include whole peppercorns, peppercorn oil, and extracts used in traditional medicine, as well as in food and pharmaceutical applications.
